title: I’m hitting a wall. Hard. The feeling that AI is moving at 200mph and I’m just stuck at the starting line.
author: Legouzi6913
selftext:
Hey everyone,

I’m posting this here because if I tell anyone in my "real life," I’ll just sound like I’m losing it. I’ve been lurking on r/AI_Trading, r/ClaudeAI, and various coding subs for weeks now, and honestly? I’m hit a breaking point.

At first, I was hyped. I have a thousand ideas for apps, trading bots, productivity tools... I see all this potential with Claude and these new dev tools and I tell myself: "This is it. This is my chance to finally build what’s in my head."

But the reality check is hitting me like a ton of bricks, and it’s brutal.

I feel completely drowned. Every time I open an IDE or start a session with Claude, my anxiety just spikes. I don't feel in control of anything. It’s like trying to drink from a firehose—too much info, too many updates, too many frameworks. My brain is saturated; I can’t seem to actually retain anything anymore. I’m just copy-pasting prompts without understanding the "why," and at the first sign of a bug, I panic.

And the worst part? The jealousy. I see people posting "Showcases" of apps they finished in three days while claiming they "don't even know how to code." I envy them so much it actually makes me feel sick. Why does it click for them and not for me? Why do I feel like the only idiot who can’t connect the dots when everyone says this stuff is "accessible to everyone" now?

I’m on the verge of burnout. I spend my nights scrolling, trying to wrap my head around it, stressing out that I’m "missing the boat." Result: zero progress, zero sleep, and I just feel like a failure.

I just need some clarity. I need to know if anyone else is in this total fog, or if I’m literally frying my brain for nothing. How do you keep from losing your mind when the goalposts keep moving?

Sorry for the vent, I just needed to get this off my chest.

comments.author: HFT0DTE
comments.body: If you didn't need to go to med school to operate on people this is pretty much how you would feel operating on someone with no idea wtf is really going on under the hood let alone at a cellular level.
